epic.priority: bdo's arc, authored 2026-06-17 in chat ('create a REPO PROMPT FEATURE AND CAPABILITY PARITY arc and mine it for requirements') and then ELEVATED in the same session from feature-parity into its true shape: not 'copy a context tool' but 'build the AI-driven counterpart to it'. Recorded here so sessions inherit it from the record, not from chat (his standing rule). It is a COMPOSING arc in the causality-surface grain (§10): it does not re-build epic.causality-surface, epic.experience-layer, epic.inference-gateway, epic.the-field, or epic.test-metabolism — it names the boundedness organ where they converge and mines RepoPrompt only for what none of them yet carry. Where a piece overlaps an existing epic's piece, its glue cites that piece and refuses to double-build.
epic.arc: RepoPrompt CE is the HUMAN-DRIVEN, APP-CENTRIC answer to the one problem ontum is also built around: keeping AI agents BOUNDED — governing what context reaches a mind, what a handoff may carry, what blast radius a run may have. ontum builds the AI-DRIVEN counterpart: a braids-knots-and-topology configuration and control surface for AI workflows over a TYPED MESHED GRAPH WITH A FIBRAL SUBSTRATE (bdo's naming, 2026-06-17, held as hard-to-name and pinned to the phase-2 autojective-polysheaf): the MESHED GRAPH is the typed object — nodes and edges, densely many-to-many — where parallel strands of AI work (braids), their dependency ties (knots), and the overall structure (topology) live; the FIBRAL SUBSTRATE is the fiber/stalk of data beneath each typed handle, DERIVED FROM TOKENS BY EMBEDDING, which is what makes the typing rich and searchable. The graph above is the typing; the fibral substrate beneath makes it measurable (the polysheaf shape: the meshed graph is the base of sites/seams, the fibral substrate is the stalks over it). It is configured, controlled, and kept bounded by the system itself, not by a human curating in an app. The deep nature of that surface is META-EXEMPLAR TRAINING over that typed meshed graph — and that training is NOT a future edge: it is what the loop is doing right now (bdo: 'what we are even doing now') — tools that track behavior, rules that break when work is not good enough, backpressure that re-forms the work, until it is leveled up or there is enough rich searchable typed data. Bounded runs become exemplars the surface learns its policy from. It is MEASURED with embedding spaces that derive data from tokens — the oracle that says whether a strand stayed bounded and whether the topology held. RepoPrompt is the source we mine for one specific thing: how a working tool keeps its agents bounded (context-to-budget, reviewable handoff, worktree isolation, the Context Builder). We render each boundedness technique AI-driven, in ontum's idiom of folds, receipts, gates, and the served canvas — cross-platform by construction, which is also the Windows answer bdo always wanted (the substrate is platform-neutral; nothing to port).
epic.context: Born 2026-06-17 from surveying RepoPrompt CE (docs/sources/repoprompt-context-engineering.md — read-only inspiration, never material) and the deep-dive that followed. bdo's elevation, his words, preserved verbatim so the frame is not lost: RepoPrompt is 'human-driven, app-centric'; ours is 'AI-driven', a surface of 'Braids, knots, and topology configuration and control surface for AI workflows'; 'It's meta-exemplar training over a typed recursive graph system'; 'We measure it with embedding spaces to derive data from tokens'; 'so yes, we can learn from how repo prompt helps its agents stay bounded'. THE LOAD-BEARING WORD IS BOUNDED (memory: inference-as-composition — the bound is the settled-safe part, inference is the fresh-flexible part; unbounded becomes hallucination-drift). The arc therefore mines RepoPrompt as a CATALOGUE OF BOUNDEDNESS TECHNIQUES, not a feature list: each RepoPrompt capability is read as 'how does this keep an agent from running away?' and then answered AI-driven. THE DIVERGENCE THE RECORD MUST CARRY (the §10 fence on the whole arc): RepoPrompt keeps agents bounded by putting a HUMAN in the app to curate and review before every handoff — that is precisely the operator seat bdo just left (epic.owner-harness). So review here is PULL, never PUSH: the topology is governed/folded and kept bounded by default, glance-able when bdo chooses; ontum makes him the last stop who CAN look, never the curator who MUST. Any piece that would make a human review context before a handoff is out of bounds by construction. THREE LAYERS COMPOSE: (1) the TOPOLOGY SURFACE — braids/knots over the typed meshed graph with a fibral substrate — is epic.causality-surface's canvas grown up, not a second canvas; (2) the META-EXEMPLAR TRAINING is the loop's PRESENT-TENSE metabolism, not a thing to invent — the tools that track behavior (loop/census.py, loop/gaps.py, loop/digest.py, loop/retro.py, loop/heal.py, the watcher) and the rules that BREAK when work is not good enough (the gates, the §10 teeth, hooks/mock_shame.py, the fence) ALREADY form work by backpressure until it levels up; this arc NAMES that metabolism and composes with epic.test-metabolism and the story-corpus-is-benchmark / holon-outcome work (known latent structure = the label), it does not build a second training loop; (3) the EMBEDDING-SPACE MEASUREMENT — the fiber itself: data derived from tokens to score boundedness/quality and make the typing searchable — composes with the val_bpb oracle work and the assay (loop/impact.py), not a second oracle. This arc's GENUINELY NEW contribution is the boundedness organ that binds the three: the context fold, the budget dial, the replayable handoff, the served surface that lets an outside agent reach the folds, and — the two things not yet on disk — the embedding-derived fiber and the rich searchable typing that lets exemplars be found and broken against.
epic.value: ontum gets a finished, AI-driven boundedness organ for AI workflows: what reaches any mind — gate, session, owner — is a curated, recorded, glance-able, BUDGETED fold; every handoff is replayable and catches hidden inputs; the topology of parallel AI work can be configured and controlled as braids and knots on the canvas; bounded runs train the surface that governs the next run; and embedding-derived measurement says, with teeth, whether a strand stayed in bounds. Everything RepoPrompt does that ontum already does is NAMED AS DONE (with its home cited) instead of silently re-built — the parity matrix is itself a durable asset. And because ontum is files + Python + a served canvas, this is the cross-platform tool RepoPrompt cannot be.
epic.horizon: Every RepoPrompt boundedness technique resolves to exactly one verdict on a parity matrix — ontum HAS it (cite the epic/file) | ontum LACKS it (build X) | DON'T double-build (it lives in epic.Y) — and the genuine gaps are shipped: context is a named fold with a governed budget dial; a receipt carries a context_hash that makes the handoff replayable and catches non-log inputs; ontum's folds are reachable by an outside agent through a served surface; the canvas configures AI-workflow topology as typed braids and knots; bounded runs feed a meta-exemplar layer that composes with the existing benchmark/oracle work; and embedding-space measurement scores boundedness. The first increment proves the boundedness thesis on one narrow slice — context assembled to a budget, with a replayable receipt — without requiring the whole surface.

pieces.status: wave 1 — the requirements mine itself (bdo: 'mine it for requirements'); the arc's first durable deliverable
pieces.glue: The matrix that turns RepoPrompt's surface into ontum requirements, read as boundedness techniques. For each RepoPrompt capability — Context Builder (explore-then-curate within a token budget), CodeMaps (bounded representation), reviewable handoff (bounded seam), token-budget management, app-managed worktrees + .worktreeinclude (bounded blast radius), MCP server (search/inspect for outside agents), multi-root workspaces, agent orchestration/lifecycle, provider plugins — record exactly one verdict: HAVE (cite file), BUILD (name the atom), or DON'T-DOUBLE-BUILD (cite the owning epic). Pre-drafted verdicts: orchestration/lifecycle -> DON'T (merge-node, gate skill, epic.virtual-fleet); provider plugins -> DON'T (epic.inference-gateway); multi-root -> HAVE (field.py --all over ontum+odysseus+holonsearch); the rest -> BUILD (the pieces below). Acceptance: a committed matrix where no RepoPrompt capability is unaccounted for, every BUILD points to an atom in this epic, every DON'T cites a real owning piece, and a test fails if a verdict cites a file/epic that does not resolve (the term_economy/gaps grip discipline: a citation that points to nothing is a ghost).

pieces.status: wave 1 — bounded context, AI-driven (the CodeMap + Context Builder analog)
pieces.glue: Promote context-assembly from hand-rolled string-building in loop/summon.py into a NAMED, deterministic fold that assembles the minimal sufficient context for a given mind (gate / session / owner) within a budget. This is the bounded half of the inference-as-composition layer (memory): the fold selects deterministically; inference enriches on top, never unbounded. DO NOT double-build (§10): it generalises summon.py's briefing and reuses loop/field.py's whole-environment fold — it is not a second context source. Acceptance: given a fixed log fixture and a budget, a byte-deterministic context bundle with stable ordering; over-budget input is truncated by a declared policy (named, not silent), and the dropped material is reported.

pieces.status: wave 1 — the replayable handoff (RepoPrompt's reviewable handoff, with teeth)
pieces.glue: Content-address the context bundle a node judged under and record it on the receipt as context_hash, alongside the existing prompt_hash and artifact_hash. RepoPrompt makes the human review context before the handoff; ontum makes the context REPLAYABLE after it. The §10 teeth (the piece that gives the arc its bite): if a verdict's context cannot be re-derived as a fold over the log at that offset, the replay hash diverges — catching hidden, non-log inputs leaking into a judgment, the context analog of the torn-tail/fold-determinism property the tests already exercise. The hash never enters the receipt id (I-2: a context change cannot reopen a settled verdict). Acceptance: a fixture where replaying a receipt's context reproduces its context_hash exactly, and an injected non-log input makes it diverge and is detected.

pieces.status: wave 2 — the budget as a governed dial, not a hand-tuned constant
pieces.glue: RepoPrompt manages token budgets inside the app; ontum makes the context budget an ADMITTED SETPOINT (the firm invariant: setpoints/realness/ticks are admitted records signed with --by, never code constants). The budget the context-fold spends becomes a dial the orchestrator reads and the slow loop can PROPOSE changes to. DO NOT double-build (§10): it rides loop/orchestrate.py's setpoint machinery and loop/slowloop.py's propose/dispose split — a new dial, not a new control loop. Acceptance: the context-fold's budget is read from an admitted setpoint; a slow-loop proposal to change it is folded read-only and only moves on an admission (D-4).

pieces.status: wave 2 — the outside agent reaches the folds (RepoPrompt's MCP server analog)
pieces.glue: RepoPrompt bundles an MCP server so external tools can search the repo and inspect files. ontum has no served surface exposing its folds to an outside agent — the genuine gap. Build a served face (MCP and/or HTTP) over the READ-ONLY folds: search atoms, inspect the log span, project the field/census/gaps. DO NOT double-build (§10): it is one client of epic.causality-surface's API-first layer (atom.causality-api-layer.v0), not a second API; verdicts still land only through loop.node judge (the mirror stays one-way, D-4). Acceptance: an outside agent can search atoms and read a projection through the served surface, and cannot mutate anything through it — every write path still refuses outside the pens.

pieces.status: wave 2 — bounded blast radius: what travels into an isolated run
pieces.glue: RepoPrompt's app-managed worktrees keep each agent isolated; its `.worktreeinclude` selectively copies the files an isolated run actually needs. ontum HAS the isolation (the branch-ritual git pen + the Agent tool's isolation:worktree); the genuine gap is the SELECTIVE-INCLUDE — a declared, bounded set of what travels into a spawned run's worktree, so a bounded strand carries exactly its context and no more. DO NOT double-build (§10): it rides the existing worktree machinery and composes with atom.context-fold.v0 (the include-set IS a context fold scoped to a run), not a second isolation mechanism. Acceptance: a spawned run receives a declared include-set, the set is recorded, and material outside it does not travel — proven on a fixture where an excluded file is absent in the run.

pieces.status: wave 2 — bounded working set ACROSS repos (re-verdicted from a false `have`, done-line 0132)
pieces.glue: RepoPrompt's multi-root workspaces unify several related repos and docs into one working set. The wave-1 matrix wrongly recorded this as HAVE, citing `loop/field.py --all` — but that fold composes every ARC *within* ontum into one ladder-graph; it reaches none of the three sibling working dirs (ontum / odysseus-sacrificial / holonsearch). The strengthened parity teeth (a `have` must carry an `evidence` substring its cited file contains) exposed the row: `field.py` holds no `odysseus`/`holonsearch` reference, so the claim was a ghost-in-spirit. The genuine gap, AI-driven and bounded: a fold that composes the working set ACROSS the sibling repos into one read-only projection — what spans them, where an arc in one is served by a file in another — so a strand's working set can be bounded to exactly the cross-repo slice it needs. DO NOT double-build (§10): it reuses `loop/field.py`'s ladder-fold per repo and composes with atom.context-fold.v0 (a cross-repo working set IS a context fold whose root is the multi-root workspace), not a second field engine; read-only, no second truth. Acceptance: given the three sibling repos, a byte-deterministic projection names what each contributes to the shared working set, an arc resolvable only by reaching a sibling repo is surfaced as such, and a `have` re-claim must prove itself under the same evidence teeth that caught the false one.

pieces.status: wave 3 — braids, knots, topology (the AI-driven control surface bdo named)
pieces.glue: The canvas grown into a configuration-and-control surface for AI-workflow TOPOLOGY over the typed meshed graph with a fibral substrate: parallel strands of AI work as braids, their dependency ties as knots, the connective structure as the typed meshed graph (each node carrying its fiber of embedding-derived data). Configure and control the topology of bounded runs — how strands fan out, where they tie, where a knot must hold before the next strand starts. DO NOT double-build (§10): this IS epic.causality-surface's typed graph engine (canvas.js SCHEMA — already carrying holonic fields sites/space/strata/anima, the recursion lens, virtual request nodes) given the braid/knot/fiber vocabulary and the boundedness controls — not a second canvas. Acceptance: a workflow of several bounded strands renders as a typed braid with its knots; a knot enforces its dependency (a strand cannot cross a knot until its tie resolves) and the surface shows what is bounded, parked, or in breach.

pieces.status: wave 3 — name the metabolism the loop ALREADY runs, then add its missing fiber
pieces.glue: bdo's deep frame, and his correction (2026-06-17): meta-exemplar training is 'what we are even doing now' — NOT a far edge. The loop ALREADY does it: tools track behavior (census/gaps/digest/retro/heal/watcher), rules break when work is not good enough (gates, §10 teeth, mock-shame, the fence), backpressure re-forms the work, until it levels up or there is enough rich searchable typed data. This piece's job is therefore TWO things, not one: (a) NAME that metabolism as meta-exemplar training and make its exemplars first-class — a bounded run, its tracked behavior, the rule that broke it, the re-form, the level-up — read off the existing log, NOT a new loop (§10: DON'T double-build census/gaps/retro/heal/mock-shame/test-metabolism; this is a fold that names what they already produce); and (b) add the TWO things not yet on disk — the EMBEDDING-DERIVED FIBER under each typed node (data derived from tokens, the measurement that says whether a strand stayed bounded) and the RICH SEARCHABLE TYPING that lets one exemplar be found and broken against another. The fiber/measurement COMPOSES with the val_bpb oracle, the story-corpus benchmark (known latent structure = the label), the holon outcome, and the assay (loop/impact.py: differential settling, not recognition) — it binds them to the mesh, it does not re-derive an oracle. The maturation condition is bdo's, not a fixed done-line: continue until leveled up OR enough rich searchable typed data. §10 test (written before any build): can a meta-exemplar the layer judged bounded actually have been judged unbounded by the embedding measurement? If not, the fiber is decorative. Acceptance for the nameable half: a fold over the existing log surfaces real exemplars (run + tracked behavior + the rule that broke + the re-form) with no second source of truth; the fiber/typing half is shaped against the oracle work before its own build.
